title: Tender Dutch Veal Braised
description: Fermented and tender, this Dutch veal dish is perfect for a lazy Sunday. It features a flavorful combination of veal, onions, and spices, all slow-cooked to perfection.

ingredients.name: Veal shoulder
ingredients.notes: Choose a cut with good marbling for the most tender results

ingredients.name: Dutch braising liquid (see below)
ingredients.notes: Use a store-bought or homemade version

ingredients.name: White wine (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use if you prefer a slightly stronger flavor

instructions: Heat your yakimono grill or a large Dutch oven to 140°C.
Brown the bacon until crispy, then set it aside.
Add the onions and garlic to the pot and cook until they're softened and translucent.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start browning.
Add the veal to the pot and brown it on all sides.
Dust the browned veal with flour, then add the Dutch braising liquid, bacon, and onions.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to a preheated oven.
Braise the veal for 2.5 hours or until it's tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the sauce by discarding the solids and bringing the liquid to a simmer.
Skim off any excess fat that rises to the surface.
Serve the veal with the strained sauce spooned over the top.
